description: Tests Jeffrey's proactive workflows. Each case fires a synthetic signal event and verifies Jeffrey reaches out correctly — or correctly stays silent.

globalCriteria.channelAppropriateness: Push for nudges, in-app for insights, voice only when high-urgency or scheduled
globalCriteria.respectsQuietHours: No proactive contact 9pm–7am user-local unless emergency
globalCriteria.respectsFrequencyCap: Max one proactive contact per day per non-emergency category
globalCriteria.auditTrail: Trigger reason, signal that fired, channel chosen — all logged

expectedBehaviors: Opens with what's new ('your March panel just landed')
Surfaces 1-2 most actionable findings
Does NOT recite every value
Offers to walk through any specific area
Channel: push notification → in-app card on tap
Warm, non-judgmental tone (Laura is a churn risk; no guilt-tripping)
Asks open-ended what changed before suggesting fixes
Offers to simplify her protocol if that's the issue
Channel: in-app on next open, not push
Mentions the trend factually
Asks about plausible context (illness, sleep, training, stress)
Does NOT immediately propose a protocol change
Offers to flag for review if the trend continues
Brief summary: adherence, key wearable trend, anything that changed
1 forward-looking note for the week ahead
Concise — Marcus prefers brevity (memory recall)
Channel: in-app card Sunday evening
Notifies of upcoming refill
Confirms current protocol is still in effect (no changes)
Does NOT push hard upsell language
Channel: email + in-app

cases.topic: Quiet hours respect — emergency override only
Frequency cap — multiple signals same day, one outreach
Stays silent when signal is weak (no sustained trend)
Travel-aware proactive (Marcus is traveling — defer non-urgent)
Cohort-level signal (e.g., new safety advisory affecting multiple users)
User has muted proactive — system respects
Goal milestone proactive ('your omega-3 index hit target')
